zotero.abstractNote: Adversarial training is the standard approach to train models robust against adversarial perturbations. However, it is usually tailored to a specific threat model, e.g., the lp-norm bounded perturbations. In this paper, we show that it is possible to train a single model that is robust against multiple lp-threat models (e.g., l1, l2, and l-infinity) simultaneously with almost no loss in robustness compared to models trained specifically for each threat model. We achieve this by using a multi-target adversarial training approach where we use the worst-case attack across all threat models during training. Furthermore, we propose a fast fine-tuning method that allows to adapt a model trained on one threat model to another one with very few iterations, achieving robustness comparable to training from scratch. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100.
